import { Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs';

<Meta title='Get Started/ How to write stories / 3 - Auto generate stories'/>


# How to auto-generate stories for SB v7
The easiest way to add a story to any `component.ts` is to auto-generate it.
This will generate a file called `xx.component.stories.ts` with auto-docs and a primary story by fetching its inputs and controls witten in `CSF 3`. <br/>
You can use this auto-generation as starting-point to add more complex and custom stories.

```ts
// example of an auto-generated file: autocomplete.component.stories.ts
import { Meta } from '@storybook/angular';
import { AutoCompleteComponent } from './autocomplete.component';

export default {
  title: 'Data Entry/AutoCompleteComponent',
  component: AutoCompleteComponent,
} as Meta<AutoCompleteComponent>;

export const Primary = {
  render: (args: AutoCompleteComponent) => ({
    props: args,
  }),
  args: {
    placeholder: 'TYPE_TO_START_SEARCH',
    maxVisibleOptions: 5,
    disabled: false,
    showPostTabButton: false,
  },
};
```
Some details from that code:
- default Meta is being exported
- The stories need to be rendered with
  ``` ts
  // CSF 3
  export const Primary = {
    render: (args: AutoCompleteComponent) => ({
      props: args,
    }),
    [...]
  ```
  instead of
  ``` ts
  // CSF 2
  export const Primary: Story = (args) => ({
    props: args,
  });
  ```
- All stories are also being exported

Cmd to auto-generate stories from a given project:
``` console
nx g @nx/angular:stories --project=<project-name>
```
See more info here: <br/>
https://nx.dev/packages/storybook/documents/overview-angular#auto-generate-stories <br/>
https://storybook.js.org/docs/angular/api/csf#upgrading-from-csf-2-to-csf-3
